mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or trigger condition shows no predictive edge in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves near-term earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO growth (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation trigger months, or inflation threshold fails to predict return direction in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Divergent labor signals produce negative returns, or the interaction effect yields a negative coefficient in predictive models over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth indicates expanding economic activity and higher corporate profit margins, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or GDP growth shows zero predictive power for quarterly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth falling below 1.0 percent year-over-year signals supply-side stagnation and weakening business investment, which reduces earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >60% of instances when industrial growth crosses below the threshold, or the threshold condition shows no statistical edge in validation.
